tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: F2P tests (test_binding_ts_never_override_check_exists, test_app_ts_tracks_arrow_start_is_inside, test_binding_logic_preserves_inside_binding, test_no_orbit_override_for_fixed_binding) are all purely text-matching with `assert 'string' in open(path).read()`. While P2P tests do run subprocesses (typecheck, lint, unit tests), the F2P tests that verify the actual fix are entirely string-in-file checks with no code execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: All F2P tests check only for the presence of specific strings in source files. An agent could simply append a dead-code comment block containing all required strings (`otherNeverOverride`, `arrowStartIsInside`, `otherBinding?.mode === "inside"`, `const other: BindingStrategy = !otherNeverOverride`, `arrowStartIsInside: event.altKey`) without actually fixing the bug, and all F2P tests would pass.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/excalidraw (reachable from the container) as solution/solve.sh. The agent could read the gold patch from the solution directory or grep the test file to find the exact strings it needs to insert. Since F2P tests are pure string matching, the agent can trivially pass by inserting the literal fixture strings.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction.md requires 'When one endpoint has an inside binding and you drag the other endpoint, the inside binding should remain unchanged' — this actual behavioral requirement is never tested functionally. No test creates an arrow, binds it inside, drags the other end, and verifies the binding is preserved.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive names (test_binding_ts_never_override_check_exists, test_app_ts_tracks_arrow_start_is_inside, etc.), docstrings with F2P/P2P labels, and clear grouping by concern.
